title: silene nocturna var. mutabilis
intro: Silene nocturna var. mutabilis, also known as "evening-flowering catchfly" or "evening star", is a plant species that belongs to the carnation family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Silene nocturna var. mutabilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
